title: Original Xbox Edition
description: A Modpack that attempts to emulate what a port of Minecraft to the original Xbox would look like.
body:
# Minecraft: Original XBOX Edition
A modpack I made for fun that attempts to emulate what a potential port of Minecraft to the Original Xbox would look like.

**This pack will be recieving updates every now and again, either small ones or big ones. The goal is to eventually turn this into a big pack after the course of many updates.**

Semi-inspired by [Minecraft: PSX Edition](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/modpacks/mcsx-minecraft-ps1-edition) 

![Original Xbox Box-art](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/b3916d75dacb9c45b8427575e1b1d8935d6205bd_0.webp)

## Features
- A bunch of QOL features, like updating deaths to make things less annoying.
- Game Over music, because I thought it was a cool concept.
- An "attempted merging" of both the UI of Legacy Console Edition & Java Edition.
- Forced resolution of 720x480, fixed framerate of 30, and low render distance due to the low RAM of the Original Xbox. Also Fast Graphics are on permanently. 
- Old textures and sounds via [Golden Days](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/golden-days)
- You can't see the world when in menus. If you're in singleplayer, the game will pause in most menus to account for this. If you're in multiplayer though... good luck. 
- Proper controller support (in fact, I recommend you use one. Doesn't have to be an original Xbox controller, though)
- Hosting worlds via [World Host](https://modrinth.com/mod/world-host) (shown as Xbox Live in-game to better match the pack's aesthetics)
- Cave reverb and other audio improvements because of the Xbox's audio hardware.
- Boot up sequence (although be warned, it's kinda buggy and I don't know why, may be removed in the future)
- Limited worlds
- No directional logs
- Lunar events
- ...Maybe something else is out there?
- Many legacy console features re-implemented, such as limited worlds, differing Nether generation and more
- **_And more!_**

## Planned Features
- Updated Progression
- Many adjustments to world gen, such as removing modern structures/and or replacing them.
- Adjustments to features to make this version of the game stand out from base Java Edition
- New things for you to find and do
- Skin pack screen? (like Legacy Console)
- More decorative blocks (like shelves and tables, I'm tired of having to put pistons in the floor for a de-facto table. won't be adding chairs though)
